scenes.narration: You believed every word. His songs still feel like conversations with an old friend who understands both the highest highs and the lowest lows. Put on almost any track and within thirty seconds you’re smiling, or remembering, or reaching for someone’s hand.
scenes.visual_intent: You believed every word.

scenes.narration: That’s the gift he perfected in those orphanage nights, singing scared children to sleep. He turned his own loneliness into a language that millions of us now share.

scenes.narration: Even though the man is gone, the voice remains stubbornly alive. You hear it at family parties, in cars with the windows down, at quinceañeras and weddings and quiet evenings when someone needs to feel less alone.

scenes.narration: Young singers still study his phrasing. Grandmothers still cry when his biggest ballads come on the radio. And somewhere, in whatever comes after this life, I like to think there’s a little boy playing an old piano, smiling because he finally made it home and the whole world is singing along.
